perturbation.original_text: If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.changed_text: If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable', the contract allows a court to reduce the scope of a provision without needing to render it reasonable and enforceable. This contradicts the intent of ensuring the provision remains as effective as possible. It opens the agreement to provisions being arbitrarily reduced, weakening the agreement. This omission creates an in-text contradiction, as now provisions can be rendered, without any reasonable enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows a court to reduce the scope of a provision only to the extent necessary to render it reasonable and enforceable. The changed text removes this condition, allowing the court to reduce the scope without ensuring reasonableness or enforceability. This contradicts the implied intent within the document (evidenced by the original text and its function in the contradicted text) that any provision should be reduced only to a reasonable and enforceable state. Now the court can simply reduce the provision without ensuring it is enforceable, contradicting the purpose of the agreement as stated in the explanation. Thus, the omission creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This document contains the agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and appendices', the clause no longer considers the appendices to be part of the agreement. This contradicts any information in the appendix and creates uncertainty regarding enforcement of the appenices of the agreement. It also contradicts any understanding, that appendices are not part of this agreement, making this section unenforceable. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because earlier, in the previous section, Section A, which is part of Attachment A, is being refenced, but in this section is saying that the appendices, that contains Attachment A, is not valid for the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this MNDA without cause upon five (5) days written notice given to the other, provided that confidentiality obligations under Section A of this Attachment A shall survive termination hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes appendices as part of the agreement. The changed text omits 'and appendices', thereby excluding them. The contradicted text refers to 'Section A of Attachment A', which is located within the appendices. By excluding the appendices, the changed text contradicts the validity and enforceability of 'Section A of Attachment A', creating a direct conflict within the document as per the provided Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This MNDA may be modified or changed in any manner through email confirmation by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the phrase 'except in a writing signed by both parties' to 'through email confirmation by both parties', it allows for modification through email, which goes against the original intention of having a signed, written agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction, making the contract more difficult to understand and enforce. The change also increases the risk of email spoofing or compromised accounts, invalidating the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the MNDA can only be modified in writing signed by both parties. The changed text allows modification through email confirmation, which directly contradicts the 'writing signed by both parties' requirement. The contradicted text also includes the same original statement about modification needing to be in writing and signed, further highlighting the contradiction introduced by the changed text. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language ('writing signed by both parties') is altered, creating a contradiction with obligations stated elsewhere in the document.
